fool.name: The Fool

meanings.upright: Represents new beginnings, a spirit of adventure, and infinite potential. The Fool is about to embark on an unknown journey, full of optimism and trust. It is a time to follow your intuition and embrace change.
meanings.reversed: Warns against being too reckless or naive. It may suggest a lack of planning, blind risk-taking, or a need to be more grounded. Reminds you to think before you act.

fool.description: A traveler stands at the edge of a cliff, carrying a small bag, ready to leap into the unknown. A small dog accompanies him, symbolizing instinct and loyalty.

magician.name: The Magician

meanings.upright: You possess all the resources and abilities needed to achieve your goals. Now is the moment to take action and manifest your ideas into reality. Believe in your own power.
meanings.reversed: May suggest self-doubt or a failure to fully utilize your talents. Be wary of deception or manipulative behavior, whether from others or yourself.

magician.description: The Magician stands before an altar, raising a wand to the sky while pointing to the ground with the other hand. Tools representing the four elements lie on the table.

highPriestess.name: The High Priestess

meanings.upright: Listen to your inner voice and intuition. The High Priestess invites you to delve deep into the subconscious to explore hidden truths. Trust your sixth sense.
meanings.reversed: You may be ignoring the guidance of your intuition and relying too much on logic. Alternatively, it suggests hidden motives and secrets that need to be revealed.

highPriestess.description: The High Priestess sits between two pillars, holding a scroll, with a crescent moon at her feet. She connects the worlds of consciousness and the subconscious.

empress.name: The Empress

meanings.upright: Symbolizes abundance, nourishment, and the flow of creativity. Focus on your sensory pleasures, connect with nature, and allow yourself to receive goodness.
meanings.reversed: May indicate over-dependence on others or neglecting your own needs. Creativity is blocked, and there is a need to reconnect with your inner feminine energy.

empress.description: The Empress sits amidst a bountiful wheat field, surrounded by the beauty of nature. She represents maternal nurturing and abundance.

emperor.name: The Emperor

meanings.upright: Establish order and structure, and exert leadership. The Emperor represents stability, discipline, and practical wisdom. Create a plan and execute it firmly.
meanings.reversed: Beware of over-control or rigidity. It may suggest issues with authority or a need to break out of old, restrictive patterns.

emperor.description: The Emperor sits upon a stone throne, holding a scepter and orb, with majestic mountains behind him. He symbolizes worldly power and stability.

hierophant.name: The Hierophant

meanings.upright: Seek traditional wisdom and spiritual guidance. May suggest a need to follow established rules or learn from a mentor. Respect traditional values.
meanings.reversed: Question tradition and find your own path. May imply breaking the rules or developing a personal, unique belief system.

hierophant.description: The Hierophant sits between two pillars, holding a scepter, with two kneeling devotees before him. He represents tradition and spiritual authority.

lovers.name: The Lovers

meanings.upright: About love, relationships, and important choices. The Lovers card suggests the need to make decisions aligned with your inner values, seeking harmony and balance.
meanings.reversed: Disharmony in a relationship or a conflict of values. You may face a difficult choice and need to re-examine what truly matters to you.

lovers.description: Adam and Eve stand in the Garden of Eden under the blessing of an angel. Symbolizes love, union, and divine choice.

chariot.name: The Chariot

meanings.upright: Achieve victory through firm willpower and determination. Control opposing forces and move toward your goals. Trust that you can overcome any obstacle.
meanings.reversed: You may feel out of control or lack direction. Setbacks remind you to re-evaluate your strategy and strengthen your inner resolve.

chariot.description: A warrior drives a chariot pulled by two opposing sphinxes, without reins yet determined. Symbolizes the victory of willpower.

strength.name: Strength

meanings.upright: True strength comes from inner courage and patience, not brute force. Face challenges with gentleness and compassion, taming the beast within you.
meanings.reversed: You may feel a lack of inner strength or self-doubt. You need to learn how to manage your emotions and avoid repression or outbursts.

strength.description: A woman gently closes the lion's mouth, taming the beast with patience and love. Symbolizes inner strength and courage.

hermit.name: The Hermit

meanings.upright: A time for solitude and reflection is needed. The Hermit guides you to look inward for answers, away from the noise of the outside world. Wisdom comes from within.
meanings.reversed: May indicate excessive loneliness or a refusal to seek help from others. It could also suggest avoiding the truth, necessitating a move out of isolation.

hermit.description: The Hermit holds a lantern, standing alone atop a mountain. Symbolizes introspection, seeking truth, and spiritual guidance.

wheelOfFortune.name: Wheel of Fortune

meanings.upright: Changes and turning points in the cycles of life. The Wheel of Fortune turns, bringing good luck and new opportunities. Adapt to change and seize the moment.
meanings.reversed: You may encounter bad luck or resist inevitable changes. Reminds you to maintain hope in adversity; this too shall pass.

wheelOfFortune.description: A giant wheel turns, with mythological creatures at the four corners. Symbolizes the cycles of fate and the constant changes of life.

justice.name: Justice

meanings.upright: A moment of fairness and balance. Face the truth and bear the consequences of your actions. Honesty and integrity will bring a just verdict.
meanings.reversed: You may face unfair treatment or need to confront dishonest behavior. Re-evaluate what is truly fair.

justice.description: The goddess of Justice holds scales and a sword, blindfolded. Symbolizes justice, truth, and the law of cause and effect.

hangedMan.name: The Hanged Man

meanings.upright: A moment for pause and reflection. Look at situations from a different perspective; a temporary sacrifice might be needed to gain greater insight.
meanings.reversed: You may be resisting necessary change or clinging to a needless sacrifice. Learn to let go at the appropriate time.

hangedMan.description: A man hangs upside down from a tree, in a peaceful posture. Symbolizes sacrifice, new perspective, and voluntary pause.

death.name: Death

meanings.upright: Significant transformation and endings, making room for new beginnings. Let go of old patterns and embrace necessary change. Death brings rebirth.
meanings.reversed: Resisting an inevitable ending leads to stagnation. Fear of change only prolongs suffering; learn to accept and let go.

death.description: A skeleton knight rides a white horse, with people kneeling before him. Symbolizes transformation, ending, and inevitable change.

temperance.name: Temperance

meanings.upright: Seeking balance and moderation. Temperance reminds you to find the middle path between extremes and to patiently harmonize different elements.
meanings.reversed: You may be in a state of imbalance or heading toward extremes. You need to find your life's equilibrium and center again.

temperance.description: An angel stands by the water, mixing liquid between two cups. Symbolizes balance, moderation, and alchemy.

devil.name: The Devil

meanings.upright: Face your shadow side and bondages. The Devil represents material desires and addictions, reminding you not to become a slave to your cravings.
meanings.reversed: Break the chains and regain your freedom. Realize that limitations are often self-imposed, and you have the power to liberate yourself.

devil.description: The Devil looks down upon a chained man and woman, but the chains are loose. Symbolizes bondage, temptation, and material desire.

tower.name: The Tower

meanings.upright: Sudden and drastic change, the collapse of old structures. Although painful, it is a necessary awakening to make room for the truth.
meanings.reversed: You may be trying to avoid an inevitable change, or experiencing a personal transformation internally rather than an external collapse.

tower.description: A tall tower is struck by lightning, with people falling from it. Symbolizes sudden change, revelation, and the collapse of old structures.

star.name: The Star

meanings.upright: A beacon of hope and inspiration. The Star brings serenity and faith, trust that the universe is guiding you. A time for spiritual connection and healing.
meanings.reversed: You may feel despair or a loss of faith. Temporary disillusionment reminds you to reconnect with your inner light and trust.

star.description: A woman pours water into a pond and onto the earth under a starry sky. Symbolizes hope, inspiration, and cosmic connection.

moon.name: The Moon

meanings.upright: The world of the subconscious and hidden truths. The Moon reveals fears and illusions but also brings profound intuition. Trust your feelings.
meanings.reversed: Secrets are revealed and illusions dissipate. Facing your fears will lead to a clear understanding.

moon.description: A full moon hangs high, with a dog and wolf howling below, and a crayfish crawling out of the water. Symbolizes the subconscious, illusion, and intuition.

sun.name: The Sun

meanings.upright: A time of light, joy, and success. The Sun brings vitality and positive energy; everything is clear and bright. Enjoy the gifts of life.
meanings.reversed: You may experience temporary gloom or blind spots caused by conceit. Success is merely delayed; maintain your faith.

sun.description: A joyful child rides a white horse, with the sun high in the sky. Symbolizes joy, success, and the vitality of life.

judgement.name: Judgement

meanings.upright: A moment of inner awakening and rebirth. Respond to your soul's calling, forgive yourself and others, and welcome new beginnings.
meanings.reversed: You may be resisting your inner calling or deep in self-doubt. Self-reflection and forgiveness are needed to move forward.

judgement.description: An angel blows a trumpet, with people rising from their graves. Symbolizes judgment, awakening, and rebirth.

world.name: The World

meanings.upright: Completion and accomplishment of a cycle. The World symbolizes integration and wholeness; you have reached a significant milestone. Celebrate your achievement.
meanings.reversed: There may be unfinished business or a difficulty in letting go. You need to find true closure before starting a new cycle.

world.description: A dancer is in a wreath, with mythological creatures at the four corners. Symbolizes completion, achievement, and cosmic unity.

wands_1.name: Ace of Wands

meanings.upright: The Ace of Wands represents a spark of inspiration and the birth of creative energy. It signals a time of new beginnings filled with enthusiasm and potential.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Ace of Wands suggests delays or a lack of motivation. You may feel hesitant to start new projects or fear that your creative potential is being wasted.

wands_1.description: A hand emerges from the clouds offering a sprouting wand, symbolizing creative inspiration and new opportunities.

wands_2.name: Two of Wands

meanings.upright: The Two of Wands indicates planning for the future and making progress toward your goals. You stand at a crossroads, contemplating your next move with confidence.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Two of Wands suggests fear of the unknown or poor planning. You may be avoiding decisions or procrastinating when you should be taking action.

wands_2.description: A figure stands on a castle wall, holding a globe and a wand, gazing out at the distant horizon.

wands_3.name: Three of Wands

meanings.upright: The Three of Wands represents expansion and exploration. Your plans are taking shape, and you're ready to venture into new territory with confidence and foresight.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Three of Wands indicates obstacles or delays in your plans. You may feel limited by circumstances or unprepared for the challenges ahead.

wands_3.description: A figure stands on a cliff watching ships sailing across the sea, representing the expansion of horizons.

wands_4.name: Four of Wands

meanings.upright: The Four of Wands celebrates a significant milestone and the joy of achievement. It represents harmony, stability, and the happiness that comes from shared success.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Four of Wands suggests a period of transition or instability. You may lack support from others or need to postpone celebrations until a more stable time.

wands_4.description: A joyful celebration beneath a floral canopy of four wands, representing harmony and happy reunions.

wands_5.name: Five of Wands

meanings.upright: The Five of Wands represents conflict, competition, and tension. Multiple forces are at play, creating a chaotic but potentially energizing environment.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Five of Wands suggests avoiding conflict or finding resolution. It's time to seek cooperation and agreement rather than continuing to compete.

wands_5.description: Five figures clash with wands in a mock battle, representing competition and the struggle of differing opinions.

wands_6.name: Six of Wands

meanings.upright: The Six of Wands heralds victory and public recognition for your achievements. Success brings confidence and the admiration of others.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Six of Wands warns against arrogance or a fall from grace. You may not receive the recognition you expect, or success may be delayed.

wands_6.description: A triumphant rider on horseback is celebrated by a crowd, crowned with a victory wreath.

wands_7.name: Seven of Wands

meanings.upright: The Seven of Wands represents standing your ground and defending your position. Despite challenges from all sides, your resilience and perseverance will see you through.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Seven of Wands suggests feeling overwhelmed or exhausted. You may be tempted to give up or feel vulnerable to attacks from competitors.

wands_7.description: A figure stands on higher ground, defending against six wands raised against them from below.

wands_8.name: Eight of Wands

meanings.upright: The Eight of Wands brings swift action and rapid progress. Things are moving quickly, and momentum is on your side. Seize the moment.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Eight of Wands indicates delays or resistance. Progress may be slowed by confusion or frustration, requiring patience.

wands_8.description: Eight wands fly through the air with great speed, symbolizing rapid movement and swift communication.

wands_9.name: Nine of Wands

meanings.upright: The Nine of Wands represents resilience and the courage to keep going despite weariness. You've been through battles but remain ready for the final challenge.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Nine of Wands warns of exhaustion or paranoia. You may be too defensive or approaching burnout from prolonged struggle.

wands_9.description: A weary but determined figure stands guard with bandaged head, holding a wand as eight others stand behind.

wands_10.name: Ten of Wands

meanings.upright: The Ten of Wands represents the burden of responsibility and hard work. You're carrying a heavy load but are near completion of your task.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Ten of Wands suggests it's time to release your burden. Delegate tasks or let go of responsibilities that are overwhelming you.

wands_10.description: A figure struggles to carry ten heavy wands toward a distant village, representing the weight of responsibility.

wands_page.name: Page of Wands

meanings.upright: The Page of Wands brings enthusiasm for exploration and discovery. A free spirit full of creative potential and eagerness to learn.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Page of Wands suggests lack of direction or immaturity. You may be procrastinating or receiving disappointing news about a creative project.

wands_page.description: A young figure holds a sprouting wand, gazing upward with curiosity and passion for adventure.

wands_knight.name: Knight of Wands

meanings.upright: The Knight of Wands charges forward with passion and daring. An adventurous spirit who takes action quickly and embraces new challenges.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Knight of Wands warns against recklessness and haste. Your anger or impulsiveness may lead to poor decisions or unnecessary delays.

wands_knight.description: A knight in armor rides a rearing horse, brandishing a wand with fierce determination and fiery energy.

wands_queen.name: Queen of Wands

meanings.upright: The Queen of Wands radiates confidence and magnetic charisma. She approaches life with optimism and determination, inspiring others with her warmth.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Queen of Wands suggests self-doubt or jealousy. You may be feeling insecure or becoming tyrannical in your pursuit of goals.

wands_queen.description: A regal queen sits on her throne holding a wand, with a black cat at her feet symbolizing mystery and intuition.

wands_king.name: King of Wands

meanings.upright: The King of Wands represents natural leadership and entrepreneurial vision. He leads with honor and inspires others to follow his bold vision.
meanings.reversed: The reversed King of Wands warns against ruthless leadership or impulsiveness. Your tyrannical behavior or inconsistency may alienate your followers.

wands_king.description: A commanding king sits on his throne, holding a flowering wand, embodying the mastery of fire energy.

cups_1.name: Ace of Cups

meanings.upright: The Ace of Cups overflows with love and emotional fulfillment. A new relationship or creative inspiration brings joy and spiritual abundance.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Ace of Cups suggests blocked emotions or emptiness. You may be repressing your feelings or experiencing emotional instability.

cups_1.description: A divine hand offers a cup overflowing with water, representing the abundance of emotion and spiritual love.

cups_2.name: Two of Cups

meanings.upright: The Two of Cups celebrates a harmonious partnership based on mutual attraction and respect. A meaningful union of hearts and minds.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Two of Cups indicates imbalance or disconnection in a relationship. A partnership may be broken or experiencing conflict.

cups_2.description: Two figures exchange cups in a loving gesture, with the caduceus of healing between them.

cups_3.name: Three of Cups

meanings.upright: The Three of Cups brings celebration and the joy of friendship. Community support and shared happiness lift your spirits.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Three of Cups warns against overindulgence or gossip. You may feel isolated from your community or caught in social drama.

cups_3.description: Three women raise their cups in celebration, dancing together in a circle of friendship and joy.

cups_4.name: Four of Cups

meanings.upright: The Four of Cups represents contemplation and apathy. You may be so focused on what you lack that you miss the opportunities being offered to you.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Four of Cups brings new awareness and acceptance. It's time to wake up from apathy and embrace the possibilities before you.

cups_4.description: A seated figure contemplates three cups while a divine hand offers a fourth from the clouds, unnoticed.

cups_5.name: Five of Cups

meanings.upright: The Five of Cups represents loss and grief. You're focused on past disappointments and the three spilled cups, missing the two that remain standing.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Five of Cups signals acceptance and the beginning of recovery. It's time to forgive, let go of regret, and move forward with hope.

cups_5.description: A cloaked figure mourns three spilled cups, unaware that two upright cups remain behind them.

cups_6.name: Six of Cups

meanings.upright: The Six of Cups brings nostalgia and sweet memories of childhood. Innocence, playfulness, and the comfort of familiar relationships.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Six of Cups warns against living in the past or remaining stuck in old patterns. Unresolved issues from childhood may need attention.

cups_6.description: Children exchange flowers and cups in a garden, representing innocence, nostalgia, and happy memories.

cups_7.name: Seven of Cups

meanings.upright: The Seven of Cups presents many choices and possibilities, but beware of wishful thinking and fantasy. Not all that glitters is gold.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Seven of Cups brings clarity and the ability to make a decision. Time for a reality check and commitment to one path.

cups_7.description: Seven cups float in the clouds, each containing different visions and temptations, representing choices and illusions.

cups_8.name: Eight of Cups

meanings.upright: The Eight of Cups represents walking away from something that no longer fulfills you. A courageous journey to seek deeper meaning and truth.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Eight of Cups suggests stagnation or fear of change. You may be avoiding the necessary journey or considering returning to the past.

cups_8.description: A figure walks away from eight stacked cups into the mountains, seeking deeper meaning and spiritual fulfillment.

cups_9.name: Nine of Cups

meanings.upright: The Nine of Cups is the wish card, representing contentment and emotional satisfaction. Your wishes are coming true; take time to feel grateful.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Nine of Cups reveals dissatisfaction or inner emptiness. External pleasures may mask a lack of true emotional fulfillment.

cups_9.description: A smiling figure sits before nine arranged cups, representing emotional satisfaction and fulfilled wishes.

cups_10.name: Ten of Cups

meanings.upright: The Ten of Cups radiates joy and family harmony. Lasting happiness and emotional fulfillment create a rainbow of blessings in your life.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Ten of Cups suggests family discord or emotional pain. There may be misalignment between family members or a sense of broken home.

cups_10.description: A loving family celebrates beneath a rainbow of ten cups, representing emotional fulfillment and domestic bliss.

cups_page.name: Page of Cups

meanings.upright: The Page of Cups brings creative inspiration and intuitive insights. A messenger of new love or artistic expression approaches.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Page of Cups suggests emotional immaturity or insecurity. You may be lost in fantasy or struggling with over-sensitivity.

cups_page.description: A young figure holds a cup with a fish peeking out, symbolizing creativity and the emergence of intuition.

cups_knight.name: Knight of Cups

meanings.upright: The Knight of Cups rides forth with romantic idealism and charm. He brings messages of love and creative opportunities.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Knight of Cups warns against unrealistic expectations or escapism. Moodiness and disappointment may follow idealistic fantasies.

cups_knight.description: A knight on a white horse holds a cup, representing the pursuit of romance and idealistic dreams.

cups_queen.name: Queen of Cups

meanings.upright: The Queen of Cups embodies deep compassion and emotional intuition. She nurtures others with unconditional love and understanding.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Queen of Cups suggests emotional manipulation or moodiness. You may be smothering others or struggling with insecurity.

cups_queen.description: A serene queen sits by the water, holding an ornate cup, embodying emotional depth and intuitive wisdom.

cups_king.name: King of Cups

meanings.upright: The King of Cups masters emotional balance and diplomatic wisdom. He offers mature counsel while maintaining composure.
meanings.reversed: The reversed King of Cups warns against emotional suppression or manipulation. Underneath the calm surface may lie volatility and moodiness.

cups_king.description: A composed king sits on his throne holding a cup and scepter, mastering the turbulent waters of emotion.

swords_1.name: Ace of Swords

meanings.upright: The Ace of Swords brings clarity and breakthrough thinking. A powerful new idea or mental clarity cuts through confusion.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Ace of Swords indicates confusion and chaos. You may lack clarity or experience miscommunication in important matters.

swords_1.description: A divine hand offers an upright sword crowned with a wreath, symbolizing mental clarity and breakthrough.

swords_2.name: Two of Swords

meanings.upright: The Two of Swords represents a difficult decision that you're avoiding. Blindfolded and balanced, you must choose between two challenging options.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Two of Swords brings the clarity to make a decision. Information becomes available that helps release the stalemate.

swords_2.description: A blindfolded figure sits holding two crossed swords, representing the need to make a difficult choice.

swords_3.name: Three of Swords

meanings.upright: The Three of Swords pierces the heart with grief and sorrow. A painful but necessary experience of heartbreak or loss.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Three of Swords signals recovery and forgiveness. You're moving on and releasing the pain of past heartbreak.

swords_3.description: Three swords pierce a heart, representing the pain of heartbreak, grief, and emotional sorrow.

swords_4.name: Four of Swords

meanings.upright: The Four of Swords calls for rest and recovery. Take time to recuperate and contemplate before returning to action.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Four of Swords warns of restlessness or burnout. Stress from lack of proper rest is hindering your progress.

swords_4.description: A knight lies in repose in a church, with three swords above and one beneath, representing rest and recuperation.

swords_5.name: Five of Swords

meanings.upright: The Five of Swords represents conflict and hollow victory. Winning at any cost may lead to isolation and betrayal of trust.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Five of Swords brings opportunities for reconciliation and making amends. Resolution of conflicts is possible now.

swords_5.description: A figure collects three swords while two defeated opponents walk away, representing conflict and its aftermath.

swords_6.name: Six of Swords

meanings.upright: The Six of Swords represents transition and moving on from troubled waters. A journey toward calmer times and healing.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Six of Swords suggests resistance to change or carrying too much baggage. You may be stuck in stagnant waters.

swords_6.description: A boatman ferries passengers across calm waters, leaving troubled times behind for a better future.

swords_7.name: Seven of Swords

meanings.upright: The Seven of Swords suggests deception or strategic cunning. You may be getting away with something or need a clever strategy.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Seven of Swords indicates a guilty conscience or being caught. It may be time to confess and face the consequences.

swords_7.description: A figure sneaks away with five swords while leaving two behind, representing deception and strategy.

swords_8.name: Eight of Swords

meanings.upright: The Eight of Swords represents self-imposed restriction and feeling trapped. Your limitations may be more mental than real.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Eight of Swords brings freedom and release. You're taking control and seeing new perspectives that liberate you.

swords_8.description: A bound and blindfolded figure stands among eight swords, representing restriction and feeling trapped.

swords_9.name: Nine of Swords

meanings.upright: The Nine of Swords represents anxiety and worry that keeps you awake at night. Mental anguish and fear are overwhelming you.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Nine of Swords brings hope and the end of despair. Reach out for help as recovery from anxiety is possible.

swords_9.description: A figure wakes in distress, head in hands, with nine swords on the wall, representing anxiety and nightmares.

swords_10.name: Ten of Swords

meanings.upright: The Ten of Swords represents a painful ending or deep betrayal. Rock bottom has been reached, but the worst is now behind you.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Ten of Swords signals recovery and regeneration. A new beginning emerges from the ashes of a painful ending.

swords_10.description: A figure lies with ten swords in their back, representing painful endings and betrayal.

swords_page.name: Page of Swords

meanings.upright: The Page of Swords brings curiosity and new ideas. A messenger of communication and mental alertness approaches.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Page of Swords warns of deception or manipulation. Bad news or gossip may be circulating that requires vigilance.

swords_page.description: A young figure stands alert holding a sword, ready to defend and observe with keen curiosity.

swords_knight.name: Knight of Swords

meanings.upright: The Knight of Swords charges forward with ambition and determination. Swift action and assertiveness drive you toward your goals.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Knight of Swords warns against impulsive aggression. Haste and ruthlessness may lead to unnecessary conflicts.

swords_knight.description: A knight charges forward on horseback, sword raised high, representing determination and swift action.

swords_queen.name: Queen of Swords

meanings.upright: The Queen of Swords combines intellect with independence. She offers unbiased judgment and communicates with clarity and directness.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Queen of Swords suggests coldness or bitterness. You may be overly emotional or cruel in your communication.

swords_queen.description: A stern queen sits on her throne under a cloudy sky, holding a sword, representing intellectual clarity.

swords_king.name: King of Swords

meanings.upright: The King of Swords embodies intellectual authority and mental clarity. He makes decisions based on truth and logical analysis.
meanings.reversed: The reversed King of Swords warns of tyranny disguised as authority. Coldness and cruelty may masquerade as intellectual superiority.

swords_king.description: A regal king sits in judgment, sword raised, representing intellectual authority and fair-minded leadership.

coins_1.name: Ace of Pentacles

meanings.upright: The Ace of Pentacles brings a new financial opportunity and the promise of prosperity. A seed of abundance is ready to manifest.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Ace of Pentacles warns of a lost opportunity or lack of planning. Scarcity or greed may block your material success.

coins_1.description: A divine hand offers a pentacle above a garden path, representing new material opportunities and prosperity.

coins_2.name: Two of Pentacles

meanings.upright: The Two of Pentacles represents the balance of multiple priorities. Stay flexible and adaptable as you juggle responsibilities.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Two of Pentacles suggests overwhelm and disorganization. Financial stress requires you to reprioritize your commitments.

coins_2.description: A figure juggles two pentacles in an infinity loop, representing the balance of competing priorities.

coins_3.name: Three of Pentacles

meanings.upright: The Three of Pentacles celebrates teamwork and skilled collaboration. Learning and implementation come together through collective effort.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Three of Pentacles indicates lack of teamwork or disregard for skills. Poor quality work may result from unresolved conflict.

coins_3.description: A craftsman works on a cathedral while others observe, representing skilled work and collaborative effort.

coins_4.name: Four of Pentacles

meanings.upright: The Four of Pentacles represents a desire for security and financial control. Conservation and frugality help protect your resources.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Four of Pentacles warns against greed and materialism. Consider being more generous rather than hoarding resources.

coins_4.description: A figure clutches four pentacles tightly, representing control over resources and desire for security.

coins_5.name: Five of Pentacles

meanings.upright: The Five of Pentacles represents financial hardship and feelings of lack. You may feel isolated during a difficult period.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Five of Pentacles signals recovery and resourcefulness. Help is available through charity or your own ingenuity.

coins_5.description: Two figures walk in the snow past a lit church window, representing hardship and spiritual isolation.

coins_6.name: Six of Pentacles

meanings.upright: The Six of Pentacles represents generosity and charitable giving. Sharing your prosperity creates balance and goodwill.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Six of Pentacles warns of selfishness or unfair exchange. You may be in debt or dealing with one-sided charity.

coins_6.description: A wealthy figure gives coins to beggars while holding a balanced scale, representing generosity and fairness.

coins_7.name: Seven of Pentacles

meanings.upright: The Seven of Pentacles represents the patience of long-term investment. Your sustained efforts are beginning to show results.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Seven of Pentacles suggests impatience or lack of growth. A bad investment or wasted effort may require reassessment.

coins_7.description: A figure pauses to contemplate growing plants and pentacles, representing assessment of long-term progress.

coins_8.name: Eight of Pentacles

meanings.upright: The Eight of Pentacles represents dedication to mastering your craft. Through repetition and apprenticeship, you develop true skill.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Eight of Pentacles warns of perfectionism or lack of focus. Your efforts may be misdirected away from true development.

coins_8.description: A craftsman diligently works on eight pentacles, representing dedication to skill development and craftsmanship.

coins_9.name: Nine of Pentacles

meanings.upright: The Nine of Pentacles represents financial independence and the enjoyment of abundance. You've achieved self-sufficiency through your efforts.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Nine of Pentacles suggests self-worth issues or showing off. You may feel dependent on others or lack true abundance.

coins_9.description: A graceful figure enjoys a garden of abundance, with a falcon on their hand, representing refined success.

coins_10.name: Ten of Pentacles

meanings.upright: The Ten of Pentacles represents lasting wealth and family legacy. Long-term success creates security for generations to come.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Ten of Pentacles warns of financial failure or family conflict. The foundation of security may be threatened by loss.

coins_10.description: A family gathers in a courtyard beneath ten pentacles, representing generational wealth and family legacy.

coins_page.name: Page of Pentacles

meanings.upright: The Page of Pentacles brings news of financial opportunity and the chance to develop new skills. Ambition drives you toward manifestation.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Page of Pentacles suggests procrastination or lack of progress. Short-term thinking may derail your ambitions.

coins_page.description: A young figure studies a pentacle with focus, representing the study of material opportunities and practical skills.

coins_knight.name: Knight of Pentacles

meanings.upright: The Knight of Pentacles approaches work with diligence and responsibility. Hard work and efficiency lead to steady progress.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Knight of Pentacles warns of laziness or obsession with details. You may be stuck in stagnation or careless with responsibilities.

coins_knight.description: A steadfast knight on a horse holds a pentacle, representing methodical progress and steady determination.

coins_queen.name: Queen of Pentacles

meanings.upright: The Queen of Pentacles nurtures others through practical care and financial security. She creates abundance and generously shares it.
meanings.reversed: The reversed Queen of Pentacles suggests work-home conflict or smothering care. You may need to focus on self-care and financial independence.

coins_queen.description: A nurturing queen sits among abundant nature, holding a pentacle, representing practical nurturing and prosperity.

coins_king.name: King of Pentacles

meanings.upright: The King of Pentacles embodies abundance and financial mastery. Through discipline and wisdom, he creates lasting prosperity and security.
meanings.reversed: The reversed King of Pentacles warns of materialism or greed. Financial incompetence or stubbornness may threaten your security.

coins_king.description: A prosperous king sits on his throne surrounded by wealth, holding a pentacle and scepter, representing material mastery.
